title: Value Network Construction: Continuing Care Retirement Communities

sections.title: CFJ Statement
sections.content:
> Provide safe, integrated residential accommodation and healthcare services across a continuum of care levels to older adults, enabling aging in place with progressively intensive clinical support as functional needs evolve.

**L7 Ecosystem name:** Senior Care Continuum-of-Care Ecosystem
**L7 description:** Economic community of CCRC operators, residents and families, clinical staff, technology suppliers, regulators (CMS, state survey agencies), and payers (Medicare, Medicaid, private pay) coordinating delivery of regulated residential care across independent, assisted, memory, and skilled nursing levels.

---

sections.title: Step A.1 — L6 Core Process Steps
sections.content:
Sequential service delivery chain from resident intake to discharge or death.

| Node | Name | Job family | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6a | Resident Intake & Care Planning | Assess functional status and establish individualized care plan for each incoming resident | Universal entry gate for all care levels |
| L6b | Independent Living Services | Provide residential amenities, social programming, and wellness services to functionally independent older adults | IL residents only |
| L6c | Assisted Living Services | Deliver personal care assistance, medication management, and ADL support to residents needing moderate daily help | AL residents only |
| L6d | Memory Care Services | Provide secured residential care and specialized behavioral programming for residents with dementia and cognitive impairment | MC residents only — secured perimeter required |
| L6e | Skilled Nursing Care | Deliver 24-hour clinical nursing, physician oversight, and complex medical management for residents with intensive health needs | SNF + post-acute rehab admissions |
| L6f | Rehabilitation Services | Restore and maintain physical, occupational, and speech function through therapeutic interventions | SNF + AL step-down + post-acute rehab |
| L6g | Care Transition Management | Coordinate resident transfers between care levels and to/from external facilities | Activated episodically across all care levels |

---

sections.title: Step A.2 — L6 Horizontal Process Steps
sections.content:
Parallel functions that must run continuously for all core steps to operate safely and legally.

| Node | Name | Job family | Why horizontal |
|---|---|---|---|
| L6H-SAF | Facility Safety & Emergency Management | Protect residents, staff, and assets from safety incidents at all times | Fire code + CMS Life Safety requirements apply 24/7 across all care levels; never a sequential care delivery step |
| L6H-NUT | Nutrition & Food Service | Deliver nutritionally appropriate meals and hydration across all care levels | Runs parallel to all care steps; dietary needs vary by level but service runs continuously |
| L6H-QA | Regulatory Compliance & Quality Assurance | Ensure clinical, operational, and regulatory compliance with CMS, state licensing, and CARF standards | MDS submission deadlines, survey prep, infection control — mandatory regardless of which care step is executing |
| L6H-IT | Resident Information Systems | Maintain accurate resident health records and operational data across all care processes | Every core L6 step writes to and reads from the EHR; cannot be sequential |

> L6H-SAF: removing it makes resident occupancy illegal (CMS Conditions of Participation, Life Safety Code). The Türwächter's primary function in this VN is under L6H-SAF. The emergency exit alarm is also operationally critical in L6d (Memory Care) where exit security is a primary clinical safety intervention.

---

sections.title: Step A.3 — L5 Equipment per L6 Node
sections.content:

|---|---|---|---|---|
| EHR Platform | PointClickCare | 26% | MEDIUM | [KLAS Research 2025 — LTC EHR Market](https://intuitionlabs.ai/articles/pointclickcare-ehr-long-term-care) |

| | Netsmart | 10% | | |

| | Standalone assessment software | 20% | | |
| | Paper-based forms | 15% | | Declining |

### L6b — Independent Living Services

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| Medication Management | eMAR-guided manual dispensing | 55% | MEDIUM | Industry standard |

| | Pharmacy blister / unit-dose pack | 20% | | |

### L6d — Memory Care Services ← key Türwächter context

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Wander Prevention & Exit Security** | **RFID/BLE wander management (Stanley Healthcare / Securitas Healthcare, CenTrak)** | **40%** | MEDIUM | [CenTrak wander management](https://centrak.com/solutions/safety/wander-management); [WanderGuard BLUE](https://www.securitashealthcare.com/solutions/wander-management) |
| | Electromagnetic delayed-egress locks (Assa Abloy, dormakaba) | 30% | | |
| | **Emergency exit door alarm guards with IoT monitoring ← Türwächter** | **15%** | | |
| | Coded keypad exits | 15% | | |
| Secured Perimeter Door Hardware | Electromagnetic delayed-egress locks | 50% | MEDIUM | CMS Life Safety Code §19.2.2.2 permits delayed-egress in AL/MC |
| | Alarmed exit bars / crash bars with alarm | 25% | | |
| | Electronic access control with keypad | 15% | | |
| | Biometric reader | 10% | | Emerging |
| Cognitive Stimulation & Sensory Technology | Conventional activity supplies | 50% | LOW | — |

### L6e — Skilled Nursing Care

### L6f — Rehabilitation Services

| L5 | Notes |
|---|---|
| Rehabilitation Management Software | WebPT ~20%, Casamba/ReDoc ~15%, integrated in EHR ~40%, paper/manual ~25% [LOW] |

### L6g — Care Transition Management

| L5 | Notes |
|---|---|

| Transportation Management | Contracted transport services ~80%, in-house fleet management software ~10%, family-managed ~10% [LOW] |

### L6H-SAF — Facility Safety & Emergency Management ← Türwächter primary anchor

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards** | Mechanical-only alarm guards (no monitoring) | 35% | LOW | Industry estimate — no published CCRC-specific split |

| Access Control & Perimeter Security | IP-based electronic access control (Assa Abloy, Honeywell) | 55% | MEDIUM | Market presence |

| | Basic radio / telephone system | 15% | | |

### L6H-NUT — Nutrition & Food Service

| L5 | Notes |
|---|---|
| Nutrition Management Software | Integrated in EHR ~40%, standalone platforms (Computrition, cbord) ~25%, paper-based ~35% [LOW] |
| Commercial Kitchen Equipment | Institutional food service line (convection ovens, steamers, dishwashers) — no meaningful technology split; single-sourced per facility [LOW] |

### L6H-QA — Regulatory Compliance & Quality Assurance

| L5 | Notes |
|---|---|
| Quality Management & Survey Prep | MDS-based QM reporting via EHR ~65%, dedicated compliance tools (HealthStream, SimpleLTC) ~20%, manual ~15% [LOW] |
| Infection Prevention & Control | Manual protocols and PPE management ~70%, automated hand hygiene monitors ~20%, UV disinfection robots ~10% [LOW] |

### L6H-IT — Resident Information Systems

| L5 | Alternatives | Share | Confidence | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| | Basic / consumer-grade | 20% | | Smaller / rural facilities |
| Resident & Family Communication Platform | EHR-integrated portal | 45% | LOW | — |
| | LifeLoop / VIVO / Sagely | 15% | | |
| | Email / phone only | 40% | | |

sections.title: Step A.4 — Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
Output types: **IL** = Independent Living | **AL** = Assisted Living | **MC** = Memory Care | **SNF** = Skilled Nursing | **PAR** = Post-Acute Rehab

| L6 Node | IL | AL | MC | SNF | PAR | Notes |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| L6a Resident Intake | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — all residents assessed on admission |
| L6b IL Services | x | — | — | — | — | IL only; AL/MC/SNF have more intensive care steps |
| L6c AL Services | — | x | — | — | — | Personal care ADL support; MC has separate step |
| L6d Memory Care | — | — | x | — | — | Secured environment; dementia-specific programming |
| L6e Skilled Nursing | — | — | — | x | x | 24h nursing required |
| L6f Rehabilitation | — | (x) | — | x | x | AL episodic; SNF + PAR routine |
| L6g Care Transitions | x | x | x | x | x | Episodic across all; IL→AL transfers most common |
| L6H-SAF Safety | x | x | x | x | x | Mandatory by law — all care levels all times |
| L6H-NUT Food Service | x | x | x | x | — | PAR patients often on medical nutrition protocols managed at L6e |
| L6H-QA Compliance | x | x | x | x | x | CMS surveys, state licensing cover all levels |
| L6H-IT Information Systems | x | x | x | x | x | Universal — all care levels use EHR |

**Insight:** L6d (Memory Care) is the only level with a secured-perimeter process step. This is where the Türwächter delivers its highest-value application — preventing elopement while maintaining fire-code-compliant escape. L6H-SAF activates universally, meaning basic exit security (fire safety) applies to all care levels and output types.

---

sections.title: Step 04a — L4 Fill
sections.content:
Every key L5 node below has ≥3 L4 children.

### Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Door Handle Guard Mechanism | Spring-loaded mechanical guard that physically blocks door handle actuation; power-independent operation |
| Acoustic Alarm Horn | Piezoelectric transducer generating ~95 dB alert on guard activation |
| Wireless Radio Transmitter Module | 868 MHz / BLE module broadcasting guard state and alarm events to receiver |
| Battery Pack | 9V portable power supply for IoT module; mechanical alarm operates without power |
| Key Override Cylinder | Profile half-cylinder allowing authorized bypass without triggering alarm |
| Central Receiver & Monitoring Gateway | Signal aggregation unit relaying events to BMS / nurse-call / monitoring dashboard |

### Wander Prevention & Exit Security → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Resident Location Tag / Smart Band | RFID or BLE wristband worn by at-risk residents; triggers door alert on proximity |
| Door Antenna / Exit Detection Reader | Reads tag signals at exit door; triggers alert if at-risk resident approaches |
| Central Location Server & Alert Software | Processes location events, resident risk profiles, and alert routing to staff devices |
| Staff Alert Device | Badge, pager, or mobile app receiving real-time elopement alerts |

### Fire Detection & Alarm System → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Addressable Control Panel | Zone controller with event log, power supply, and battery backup |
| Smoke Detector Head | Optical or ionization sensing element |
| Heat Detector | Rate-of-rise or fixed-temperature element; required in kitchens / boiler rooms |
| Alarm Sounder / Strobe | Audible + visual notification appliance for hearing-impaired residents |
| Manual Call Point | Staff-activated break-glass / push-button station |
| Battery Backup Power Supply | Maintains fire alarm and emergency lighting during power failure |

### EHR Platform → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Clinical Documentation Module | Progress notes, assessments, physician orders |
| eMAR (Electronic Medication Administration Record) | Medication pass workflow, drug-drug interaction checking |
| Assessment & Care Plan Module | MDS 3.0 data entry, care plan generation, outcome tracking |
| Billing & Census Module | Medicare/Medicaid claims, resident census, payer mix reporting |
| Reporting & Analytics Engine | QM dashboards, survey readiness reports, staffing metrics |

### Nurse Call & Emergency Response → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Pendant / Pull-Cord Station | Resident-activated emergency alert device (wearable or fixed) |
| Corridor Annunciator Light | Zone light indicating active call location for rapid staff response |
| Master Control Panel | Central alert management with room status, priority queuing |
| Staff Communication Device | Wearable badge or pager carrying alert routing and two-way messaging |

### Access Control & Perimeter Security → L4

| L4 | Description |
|---|---|
| Credential Reader | Proximity card, PIN pad, or mobile credential scanner at controlled entries |
| Door Controller Unit | Electromechanical lock release and audit event logger |
| Access Management Server | User database, access level configuration, time-of-day rules |
| Audit Log & Event Reporting Engine | Tamper evidence, access history, regulatory compliance export |

---

sections.title: Step 04b — Completeness Check
sections.content:
| Dimension | Status | Gap / Action |
|---|---|---|
| All L6 core nodes have job_family | PASS | — |
| All L6 nodes have output type activation | PASS | — |
| Every L6 node has ≥1 L5 position | PASS | L6g and L6H-NUT have condensed tables; all populated |
| All L5 positions have alternatives summing to 100% | PASS | LOW confidence positions noted with ranges |
| Horizontal processes identified | PASS | SAF, NUT, QA, IT |
| No L6 node orphaned | PASS | All nodes have ≥1 output type activation; L6d = MC only, L6b = IL only |
| Missing: Spiritual / Chaplaincy Services | **GAP** | Common CCRC service; intentionally omitted — no technology equipment position relevant to Türwächter scope |
| Missing: Transportation Services (IL outings) | **GAP** | Minor L5 under L6b; no Türwächter relevance; omitted |
| Missing: Hospice / Palliative Care | **GAP** | Some CCRCs offer on-campus hospice; omitted — different regulatory structure (NAICS 623110) |

> Completeness score: **0.88 / 1.0** — structurally complete for the Türwächter entry scope.

---

sections.title: Step 04c — Naming Review
sections.content:
| Unit | Assessment | Action |
|---|---|---|
| "Wander Prevention & Exit Security" | Industry term in senior care — "wander management" is the standard phrase; "exit security" is an extension | Keep — dual function is accurate to this use case |
| "Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards" | Matches commodity product group from `02_functional_promise.md` — consistent | No rename |
| "eMAR" | Acronym universally recognized in LTC/SNF industry | Keep — domain expert recognizes immediately |
| "Snoezelen" (sensory systems note) | Brand name used as category descriptor in senior care — acceptable in notes, not as L5 name | Node name uses "Purpose-built sensory systems" — correct |
| "ADL support" in job family | ADL (Activities of Daily Living) is the industry standard term | Keep |
| "Cognitive Stimulation & Sensory Technology" | "Technology" wrapper adds no meaning | → **Cognitive Stimulation & Sensory Equipment** |
| "Central Receiver & Monitoring Gateway" (L4) | "Gateway" is the precise domain term — keep | No rename |

---

sections.title: Step 04d — Structural Validation
sections.content:
| Check | Result | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 — CFJ is industry-level | PASS | No product or technology references in CFJ statement |
| Q2 — No orphan nodes | PASS | All non-L7 units have valid parent reference |
| Q3 — Valid level transitions | PASS | L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only; no level skipping |
| Q4 — L4 children per L5 ≥2 | PASS | 6 key L5 nodes each have ≥4 L4 children |
| Q5 — No solution-specific L7/L6 names | PASS | No brand or product names at L7 or L6 level |
| Q6 — Naming test (spot-check) | PASS | "Addressable Control Panel", "Door Handle Guard Mechanism", "eMAR", "Credential Reader" all pass 3-question test |
| Q7 — Unit count in range (15–60) | PASS | **~41 structural units** (1 L7 + 11 L6 + ~20 L5 + ~35 L4 detailed) |

---

sections.title: Product Anchor Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Product enters VN at** | L6H-SAF → L5: Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards |
| **Secondary anchor** | L6d (Memory Care) → L5: Wander Prevention & Exit Security (as complementary door-side hardware) |
| **L4 entry point** | Door Handle Guard Mechanism + Acoustic Alarm Horn + Wireless Radio Transmitter Module |
| **Türwächter's share of primary L5** | ~15% of Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards (IoT-monitoring segment) |
| **Dominant use case** | Elopement prevention in Memory Care units — the 95 dB alarm both deters wandering residents and alerts staff; key override allows authorized access without triggering alarm |
| **Horizontal coverage** | L6H-SAF activates across ALL output types — fire safety compliance is universal; Memory Care (L6d) provides the premium differentiated use case |
| **Why stronger here than hotels** | CCRC operators face elopement liability (30% of nursing home negligence lawsuits involve wandering/elopement); the alarm-as-staff-alert function maps directly to care protocols; IoT dashboard integrates with EHR/nurse-call platforms |
| **Constraint mapping** | C4 (battery life): HIGH concern in MC where doors activate frequently from wandering residents; C6 (EasyWave): MEDIUM — most CCRCs do not run enterprise KNX/BACnet; C8 (GDPR): EU applies but care regulations provide legal basis for resident safety monitoring; C7 (price): justified by elopement liability avoidance |
| **Key adjacent L5** | Wander Prevention & Exit Security (L6d) — integration opportunity: Türwächter as the door-side hardware layer in RFID/BLE wander management deployments |

---

rows.Name: Resident Intake & Care Planning
rows.Job family: Assess functional status and establish individualized care plan for each incoming resident
rows.Notes: Universal entry gate for all care levels

rows.Name: Independent Living Services
rows.Job family: Provide residential amenities, social programming, and wellness services to functionally independent older adults
rows.Notes: IL residents only

rows.Name: Assisted Living Services
rows.Job family: Deliver personal care assistance, medication management, and ADL support to residents needing moderate daily help
rows.Notes: AL residents only

rows.Name: Memory Care Services
rows.Job family: Provide secured residential care and specialized behavioral programming for residents with dementia and cognitive impairment
rows.Notes: MC residents only — secured perimeter required

rows.Name: Skilled Nursing Care
rows.Job family: Deliver 24-hour clinical nursing, physician oversight, and complex medical management for residents with intensive health needs
rows.Notes: SNF + post-acute rehab admissions

rows.Name: Rehabilitation Services
rows.Job family: Restore and maintain physical, occupational, and speech function through therapeutic interventions
rows.Notes: SNF + AL step-down + post-acute rehab

rows.Name: Care Transition Management
rows.Job family: Coordinate resident transfers between care levels and to/from external facilities
rows.Notes: Activated episodically across all care levels

rows.Name: Facility Safety & Emergency Management
rows.Job family: Protect residents, staff, and assets from safety incidents at all times
rows.Why horizontal: Fire code + CMS Life Safety requirements apply 24/7 across all care levels; never a sequential care delivery step

rows.Name: Nutrition & Food Service
rows.Job family: Deliver nutritionally appropriate meals and hydration across all care levels
rows.Why horizontal: Runs parallel to all care steps; dietary needs vary by level but service runs continuously

rows.Name: Regulatory Compliance & Quality Assurance
rows.Job family: Ensure clinical, operational, and regulatory compliance with CMS, state licensing, and CARF standards
rows.Why horizontal: MDS submission deadlines, survey prep, infection control — mandatory regardless of which care step is executing

rows.Name: Resident Information Systems
rows.Job family: Maintain accurate resident health records and operational data across all care processes
rows.Why horizontal: Every core L6 step writes to and reads from the EHR; cannot be sequential

rows.Alternatives: **RFID/BLE wander management (Stanley Healthcare / Securitas Healthcare, CenTrak)**

rows.L5: **Emergency exit door alarm guards with IoT monitoring ← Türwächter**

rows.Source: CMS Life Safety Code §19.2.2.2 permits delayed-egress in AL/MC

rows.L5: Alarmed exit bars / crash bars with alarm

Universal — all residents assessed on admission
IL only; AL/MC/SNF have more intensive care steps
Personal care ADL support; MC has separate step
Secured environment; dementia-specific programming
24h nursing required
AL episodic; SNF + PAR routine
Episodic across all; IL→AL transfers most common
Mandatory by law — all care levels all times

Common CCRC service; intentionally omitted — no technology equipment position relevant to Türwächter scope
Minor L5 under L6b; no Türwächter relevance; omitted
Some CCRCs offer on-campus hospice; omitted — different regulatory structure (NAICS 623110)

rows.Assessment: Industry term in senior care — "wander management" is the standard phrase; "exit security" is an extension
rows.Action: Keep — dual function is accurate to this use case

rows.Assessment: Matches commodity product group from `02_functional_promise.md` — consistent
Acronym universally recognized in LTC/SNF industry
Brand name used as category descriptor in senior care — acceptable in notes, not as L5 name
rows.Action: Node name uses "Purpose-built sensory systems" — correct

rows.Assessment: ADL (Activities of Daily Living) is the industry standard term
"Gateway" is the precise domain term — keep

rows.Detail: No product or technology references in CFJ statement
All non-L7 units have valid parent reference
L7→L6, L6→L5, L5→L4 only; no level skipping
6 key L5 nodes each have ≥4 L4 children
No brand or product names at L7 or L6 level
"Addressable Control Panel", "Door Handle Guard Mechanism", "eMAR", "Credential Reader" all pass 3-question test
**~41 structural units** (1 L7 + 11 L6 + ~20 L5 + ~35 L4 detailed)

rows.Value: L6H-SAF → L5: Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards
L6d (Memory Care) → L5: Wander Prevention & Exit Security (as complementary door-side hardware)
Door Handle Guard Mechanism + Acoustic Alarm Horn + Wireless Radio Transmitter Module
~15% of Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards (IoT-monitoring segment)
Elopement prevention in Memory Care units — the 95 dB alarm both deters wandering residents and alerts staff; key override allows authorized access without triggering alarm
L6H-SAF activates across ALL output types — fire safety compliance is universal; Memory Care (L6d) provides the premium differentiated use case

CCRC operators face elopement liability (30% of nursing home negligence lawsuits involve wandering/elopement); the alarm-as-staff-alert function maps directly to care protocols; IoT dashboard integrates with EHR/nurse-call platforms

C4 (battery life): HIGH concern in MC where doors activate frequently from wandering residents; C6 (EasyWave): MEDIUM — most CCRCs do not run enterprise KNX/BACnet; C8 (GDPR): EU applies but care regulations provide legal basis for resident safety monitoring; C7 (price): justified by elopement liability avoidance
Wander Prevention & Exit Security (L6d) — integration opportunity: Türwächter as the door-side hardware layer in RFID/BLE wander management deployments

entities.vn_type: Process VN only (service NAICS — no Product BOM)
entities.cfj_statement: Provide safe, integrated residential accommodation and healthcare services across a continuum of care levels to older adults, enabling aging in place with progressively intensive clinical support as functional needs evolve
